intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Perfect. The skill recipe gives me the exact pattern for this GEO loose-triplet layout. Now let me execute the workflow:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os
import pandas as pd

sc.settings.figdir = '.'  # harvested cwd, not figures/

# Load the 10x matrix triplet (GEO loose layout, non-standard names)
D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

print(f"Loading matrix triplet with prefix: {pre}")
adata = sc.read_mtx(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes×cells → transpose
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values
adata.var_names_make_unique()

print(f"Loaded: {adata.n_obs} cells × {adata.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Gene name examples: {list(adata.var_names[:5])}")
